level_signals: Owns end-to-end product surface (orchestration, provisioning, review layer)
Expected to align team around direction without heavy process

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have shipped developer tools, infrastructure products, or AI-powered applications as a product manager.
You understand distributed systems well enough to have real conversations with engineers about orchestration, sandboxing, and reliability.
You have strong product intuition for developer experience. You know when something feels right and when it doesn't.
You are deeply technical. You don't need to write production code, but you can read a PR, reason about an architecture diagram, and form opinions about tradeoffs.
You are comfortable making decisions with incomplete information and iterating fast.
You communicate clearly—written and verbal—and can align a team around a direction without a 40-page PRD.
You use AI coding tools regularly and have strong opinions about what's working and what isn't.

scope.surface: Cloud Agents product — agent provisioning, orchestration, monitoring, artifact/review layer, task handoff UX, enterprise deployment model
scope.business_model: B2B and B2D (developer-facing SaaS with enterprise/self-hosted tier)
core_responsibilities: Designing the task handoff experience end-to-end: how a developer describes what they want, how much context the agent receives, how the system handles ambiguity, and how the developer moves on without worrying about it.
Owning the agent orchestration model: sandboxing, parallelism, retries, error recovery, and resource allocation.
Building the artifact and review layer: agents return logs, screenshots, video recordings, and live previews.
Shaping the self-hosted and enterprise deployment model for customers needing agents inside their network.
Instrumenting success: task completion rate, time-to-value, cost-per-task, developer trust.
Defining how cloud and local agents hand off to each other.

green_flags: Clear and specific product surface with concrete example projects listed
Mission-driven company at the frontier of AI-assisted software development
Flat organization with small, talent-dense team — high ownership signal
Explicit metrics listed (task completion rate, time-to-value, cost-per-task, developer trust) showing measurement culture
JD is direct and avoids excessive corporate buzzwords
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated — work arrangement is completely unclear
No visa or work authorization information provided
No years-of-experience requirement stated, making seniority level ambiguous
#LI-DNI tag suggests role may not be actively sourced on LinkedIn, limiting visibility

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from scope of ownership and team description, not stated explicitly)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description — developer SaaS with enterprise tier)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (taken verbatim from JD but labeled as metrics the PM would build, not stated KPIs for the role)
location.type (unclear — no location or remote policy mentioned anywhere in JD)
compensation.equity_mentioned (set to false as no equity or comp details appear)
ambiguities: Location/work arrangement is entirely unstated — the JD contains no city, remote, or hybrid information. Set to 'unclear'.

'You may be a fit if' framing could imply these are soft preferences rather than hard requirements, but the language is unconditional and these appear to be the only stated qualifications — treated as must_haves with severity: hard.
No explicit years-of-experience requirement; seniority inferred from scope only.
